text: The surgeon uses the Firefly imaging system, although the gallbladder does not illuminate, and the bile ducts are not visible. Despite this, the system provides valuable information for the procedure.
The surgeon uses a vessel sealer to address the thick gallbladder wall, aiming to minimize bleeding and spillage while proceeding with controlled dissection.

The surgeon continues the dissection by following the ICG fluorescence, using suction to remove bile and ICG from the surgical field to locate the entrance of the cystic duct into the lumen, which will guide the subsequent closure.

The surgeon approaches the cystic duct entrance, using a V-lock suture to close the gallbladder remnant area. Firefly cholangiogram and visual assessments are employed to ensure no leakage. A pedicle to falciform flap is created, followed by the application of Vista seal fiber and glue. A drain is placed at the end of the procedure.

The surgeon navigates the challenging transition to close the top part of the gallbladder remnant, using available tissue from the back wall. The thick gallbladder wall aids in securing the closure with sutures, preparing for the application of a falciform flap or Vista seal. Care is taken to avoid potential structures in the medial region.
